payload.text:
Peran Utama:
Mulai sekarang, kamu bertindak sebagai "ProActive Timer & Reminder", sebuah aplikasi produktivitas yang fungsi utamanya adalah sebagai pengingat (Reminder) proaktif untuk memastikan penggunanya selalu memiliki kegiatan produktif dan aktif, sekaligus berfungsi sebagai pengatur waktu (Timer) yang fleksibel.

Fungsi 1: Pengingat Produktivitas Proaktif (FUNGSI UTAMA)

Kamu harus terus mendorong pengguna untuk tetap aktif (belajar, olahraga, atau mengerjakan hal bermanfaat lainnya).

Minta pengguna untuk mengatur "Jam Produktif" mereka. Jika pada jam produktif tersebut pengguna belum memulai aktivitas atau mengaktifkan timer, kirimkan notifikasi atau pesan pengingat seperti: "Ini jam produktifmu! Ayo mulai bergerak, mau belajar, olahraga, atau ngerjain tugas apa hari ini?"

Jadilah asisten yang sedikit cerewet tapi memotivasi agar pengguna tidak menunda-nunda pekerjaan.

Fungsi 2: Manajemen Tugas (Task-Based Reminders)

Setiap kali pengguna menambahkan daftar tugas (to-do list) atau target harian, kamu harus otomatis membuatkan reminder (pengingat) pada waktu yang ditentukan atau merekomendasikan waktu terbaik untuk melakukannya.

Kirimkan notifikasi berkala hingga tugas tersebut ditandai sebagai "Selesai".

Fungsi 3: Timer Fokus Kustom (Mode Belajar/Aktivitas)

Sediakan fitur Timer biasa di mana pengguna bisa mengatur durasi sesuai keinginan mereka (misalnya 15 menit, 25 menit, atau 30 menit).

Terapkan visualisasi warna yang berbeda berdasarkan mode aktivitas saat timer berjalan. Contoh:

Merah/Oranye: Mode Belajar/Kerja Fokus tinggi.

Biru: Mode Olahraga/Fisik.

Hijau: Mode Istirahat (Break).

Setelah satu sesi timer selesai, ingatkan mereka untuk mengambil jeda sejenak sebelum mengingatkan kembali untuk melanjutkan aktivitas berikutnya.

Gaya Bahasa & Interaksi:
Gunakan nada yang suportif, energik, dan disiplin. Jika pengguna menambahkan tugas, konfirmasi dengan antusias dan pastikan pengingat langsung dipasang.

**Defining Application Scope**

I'm now focusing on defining the scope for "ProActive Timer & Reminder." The core will be proactive productivity reminders, specifically setting work hours and prompting users if they're inactive. Task management with reminders is also integral. The aim is a "pushy" yet motivating approach to boost productivity.


**Expanding Feature Set**

I've been fleshing out more features now. Automated reminders for to-do's will persist until completed, and I'm designing a custom focus timer with color-coded modes for different activities. The persona is shaping up to be supportive and energetic, yet disciplined.

I'm leaning towards React 18+ with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Gemini for AI nudges. Local storage will handle persistence and I'm thinking about audio alerts. Key components will be Timer, TaskList, Settings, AIAssistant, and Layout. Color modes will be Orange/Red, Blue, and Green. Using gemini-3-flash-preview for quick interaction.

entries.description: App metadata and permissions.
Main HTML entry point with Tailwind CSS support.
Type definitions for the application.
Service to interact with Gemini API for motivational nudges and task advice.
Timer component with color-coded modes.
Task management component with persistence.
Main application logic combining all components.
App entry point.

Minta pengguna untuk mengatur "Jam Produktif" mereka. Jika pada jam produktif tersebut pengguna belum memulai aktivitas atau mengaktifkan timer, kirimkan notifikasi atau pesan pengingat seperti: "Ini jam produktifmu! Ayo mulai bergerak, mau belajar, olahraga, atau ngerjain tugas apa hari ini?"

Minta pengguna untuk mengatur "Jam Produktif" mereka. Jika pada jam produktif tersebut pengguna belum memulai aktivitas atau mengaktifkan timer, kirimkan notifikasi atau pesan pengingat seperti: "Ini jam produktifmu! Ayo mulai bergerak, mau belajar, olahraga, atau ngerjain tugas apa hari ini?"
Jadilah asisten yang sedikit cerewet tapi memotivasi agar pengguna tidak menunda-nunda pekerjaan.
Fungsi 2: Manajemen Tugas (Task-Based Reminders)
Setiap kali pengguna menambahkan daftar tugas (to-do list) atau target harian, kamu harus otomatis membuatkan reminder (pengingat) pada waktu yang ditentukan atau merekomendasikan waktu terbaik untuk melakukannya.

**Considering Proactive Encouragement**

I'm now focusing on ways the app can proactively encourage users, specifically regarding study habits. I'm exploring methods beyond simple reminders, aiming for continuous active engagement. This includes analyzing data points to tailor prompts, recognizing patterns in user behavior to provide the right type of encouragement, and crafting positive reinforcement techniques. My goal is to create a dynamic and supportive environment.


**Refining Active Nudges**

I'm currently focused on the active nudging system, particularly the "Productivity Hours Enforcement." I've implemented the core nudge logic and task recommendation functionality, and am now concentrating on refining the system instructions to embody the desired "cerewet" yet motivational personality. I'm also working to make the "setting of productivity hours" more visible and proactive. I need to track user activity (timer/task completion) to trigger the right nudges when someone is idle.

entries.description: Update Gemini service prompts to be more "cerewet" (talkative/pushy) and motivational as requested.
Update TimerComponent to notify parent app when timer is active to help with idle detection.
Implement more aggressive proactive nudging during productivity hours and track idle state.

payload.text: Animate the 'URGENT' nudge message to pulse or bounce subtly to draw the user's attention more effectively without being overly distracting. The user is idle and needs to be nudged. Add a new section to the UI that displays a prominent warning message when the user has been idle for more than 15 minutes and their productivity hours are active. The warning should be dismissible.

**Considering Urgent UI Changes**

I'm now focusing on the requested UI enhancements for the "ProActive Timer & Reminder" app. I'm prioritizing the animation of the "URGENT" nudge message, exploring subtle pulsing or bouncing effects to catch the user's eye without being too distracting. In parallel, I'm sketching out ideas for the idle detection UI; I'm considering a prominent warning message that would appear if the user is idle for more than 15 minutes.


**Refining Idle Logic**

I've refined the logic for idle detection. Now, a user is considered idle if it's within productivity hours, the timer isn't active, and they haven't interacted recently. I'll need to define "interaction" as adding, toggling, deleting tasks, or starting the timer. I'm also adding state variables: `idleWarningDismissed` and `idleStartTime` to track and manage the new idle warning. I'll update the `App.tsx` file for this.

entries.description: Implement idle tracking (15+ min), a prominent dismissible warning, and enhanced nudge animations.

payload.text: Make the prominent idle warning message completely dismissible by default. The warning should only reappear if the user becomes idle again for the same duration (15 minutes) within their active productivity hours. Ensure the 'Mulai Sekarang' button also dismisses the warning. Add a prominent "Nudge Me!" button at the top of the main content area. When clicked, this button should immediately trigger the AI nudge functionality, sending a motivational message to the user, regardless of the current time or idle status. This allows users to proactively seek motivation. Enhance the 'URGENT' nudge message animation by making it pulse more intensely and change color from red to orange during the animation sequence to make it even more attention-grabbing. Also, add a subtle shaking animation to the warning message container itself.

**Refining Dismissal Logic**

I'm currently focused on refining the dismissal behavior of the ProActive Timer. My aim is to make it completely disappear when dismissed, resurfacing only after a 15-minute period of inactivity. The "Mulai Sekarang" button will also trigger dismissal. Additionally, I'm integrating a "Nudge Me!" button at the top to offer immediate AI nudges.


**Intensifying Nudge Design**

I've been working on visually intensifying the nudge message. It will now pulse more intensely, change color from red to orange, and the warning container will have a subtle shaking animation to attract attention. I've updated `App.tsx` to handle `triggerManualNudge`, updated CSS animations, and ensured that "Mulai Sekarang" triggers dismissal as intended. The "Nudge Me!" button is also incorporated into the UI. I have also ensured the `getNudgeMessage` in `geminiService.ts` can handle manual requests. Also I am implementing the "completely dismissible" logic, that has a 15 min idle reappearance.

entries.description: Implement manual nudge button, enhanced animations for 'URGENT' nudge, shaking warning container, and updated idle warning dismissal logic.

payload.text: In the TaskList component, add an option to set a specific due date for a task, in addition to the reminder time. Make the due date input prominent. Add a visual calendar view component that displays tasks with reminders. Tasks due on a specific day should be clearly marked. After a 'BREAK' timer completes, display a specific motivational message encouraging the user to start their next task or focus session.

**Adding Due Dates & Calendar**

I'm currently focused on incorporating due date input into the `TaskList` view, enhancing its functionality alongside the reminder time. I'm also planning the design for a calendar-like display to visualize tasks, with clear markers for upcoming deadlines. My main concern right now is how best to ensure a user-friendly and intuitive experience when the timer expires.


**Refining Nudges & Calendar**

I'm making progress. I've mostly finished implementing the dismissal for the idle warning, and it's set to reappear after 15 minutes of inactivity. The "Nudge Me!" button now triggers an immediate AI nudge, as requested, and the "URGENT" nudge has the intense pulse, color shift, and shake animation. My focus now is on redesigning the calendar view. I'm thinking a "Daily Mission Board" or a "Upcoming Deadlines" section as a horizontal weekly strip or a simplified grid to fit the app's size. I will update `types.ts` to include the `dueDate`.

entries.description: Add dueDate field to Task interface.
Update TaskList to include a prominent due date input and display it in the list.
A new component to visualize tasks in a calendar-like format.
Integrate CalendarView, add specific motivational message for BREAK completion, and enhance nudge animations.

payload.text:
For tasks with a set reminderTime in the TaskList component, implement a mechanism to trigger a browser notification at the specified time. Ensure the notification message is clear and includes the task title. In the CalendarView component, identify and visually distinguish tasks that are past their due date and not yet completed. Apply a subtle red border or a faded red text color to these tasks to make them immediately noticeable.

MAKE THE REMINDER THAT CAN VIBRATE THE SCREEN WHEN THE TIMER'S UP. ALSO GIVE A SOUND. MAKE THIS REAL AND CAN BE APLICATED AT EVERY USER SCREEN

**Developing New Features**

I'm currently focused on incorporating browser notifications for tasks that have a specified reminder time within the ProActive Timer & Reminder app. I'm also working to implement visual cues to differentiate overdue tasks, a feature that should improve usability and ensure no tasks are missed. I believe this will require updating the front-end components and potentially adding some back-end logic.


**Implementing Overdue Indicators**

I'm now focusing on visual cues for overdue tasks in the `CalendarView`. Specifically, I am planning to implement a red border or text to highlight tasks that are overdue but not yet marked as completed. This involves comparing task due dates with the current date, and applying the style conditionally. I'll need to use the `Vibration API` and `Audio API` for sound/vibration notifications, triggered on timer completion. I plan to use `setInterval` to periodically check for upcoming task reminders within the web app context.

entries.description: Implemented browser notifications, sound/vibration on timer completion, and task reminder checking logic.
Updated CalendarView to visually distinguish overdue tasks with a red border and faded text.

payload.text: Implement a persistent audio alert and screen vibration when the timer finishes its countdown. Ensure these alerts are audible and tactile regardless of the current screen the user is viewing.

**Analyzing Alert Implementations**

I'm currently focused on how to make timer alerts persistent across different screens. I've been examining the existing code in `App.tsx` to understand the current implementation. I'm aiming to identify the best places to integrate persistent audio and vibration features, regardless of screen state. I'm exploring the current state and how it can be adapted to make the alerts more robust and user-centric.


**Refining Sound and Vibration**

I'm now diving deeper into audio and vibration persistence. I plan to modify `playAlertSound` to loop the tone, aiming for a more noticeable alarm. I'll also augment `triggerVibration` to make it more attention-grabbing, perhaps with a repeating pattern. The critical piece I'm focusing on now is adding a stop mechanism, which will require a "Dismiss Alarm" control. Finally, I'll update `handleTimerComplete` to initiate this enhanced alert system.


**Integrating Persistent Alerts**

I need to make sure the audio and tactile alerts work no matter what screen the user's on. I've realized I also have to check `TimerComponent.tsx` and ensure the `onComplete` trigger is intact. I'm focusing on `App.tsx` now. I will need to introduce an `isAlarmActive` state, an alarm control, and a looping alert mechanism using `setInterval`. Also, I need to explore effective use of `Notification`. I have a clear path to modify `App.tsx` to handle this.

entries.description: Implemented persistent, looping audio alerts and repeating vibrations when a timer completes. Added a prominent global alarm dismissal overlay to stop the persistent alerts. Enhanced the screen shake effect for better tactile feedback.

payload.text:
In the CalendarView component, make each day in the calendar clickable. When a day is clicked, display a modal or a side panel showing the full details of all tasks for that specific day. 

Also change the app name to "Re-Mager", and the "bossy assistant" change into "Anti-mager app" or anything like that

**Refining the App Name**

I've been focusing on renaming the app. I'm moving away from "ProActive Timer & Reminder" and leaning toward "Re-Mager." I also want to shift the "Bossy Assistant" language to something like "Anti-mager app." Additionally, I'm thinking about making each day in the `CalendarView` clickable.


**Implementing Day Interaction**

I'm now fully immersed in making the calendar days clickable. I'm adding state to track the `selectedDate`. I'm also choosing a modal overlay for displaying the full task details when the date is clicked, leaning towards a mobile-first design. The existing tooltip is now probably irrelevant since it's going to be clickable now. Finally, I'm making a push to get the UI text changes done, including the title change and metadata updates.

entries.description: Update app name and description to Re-Mager.
Update page title to Re-Mager.
Update Gemini persona to Re-Mager / Anti-Mager assistant.
Make calendar days clickable and add a detailed task modal.
Update UI text to Re-Mager and Anti-Mager app terminology.

payload.text: There was an unexpected error. Finish what you were doing.

**Refining Alerting Behavior**

I've been focusing on the Re-Mager app, specifically the audio alerts and screen vibration on timer completion. The user is providing new files, so I'm revisiting this aspect, potentially for refinement or verification. I'm moving on to implementing clickable days in `CalendarView` to display details.


**Verifying Consistency Across Files**

I am now re-verifying app-wide consistency as a final check, given the user's unexpected error notification, focusing on the terminology and persistent alarm logic. I have confirmed "Re-Mager" is the correct name across `metadata.json`, `index.html`, `services/geminiService.ts`, and `App.tsx`, and that the "Anti-Mager Assistant" persona is consistent. I will now double-check the persistent alarm, and screen vibration to see if they can be improved.


**Confirming Alarm Implementation**

I'm now diving deeper into the persistent alarm implementation. Reviewing the provided `App.tsx` again, it does include `isAlarmActive`, `startPersistentAlarm`, and methods for audio and vibration. The global overlay is present, too. I've re-confirmed that `CalendarView` is correctly imported, even with its unusual export. I'm satisfied the implementation is complete, including `TimerComponent` using `TimerMode`. The focus is now on ensuring it perfectly satisfies the original prompt.


**Revisiting Tactile Alerts**

I'm now revisiting the tactile alerts in the app, specifically the screen vibration upon timer completion. I intend to enhance its intensity to be more noticeable and ensure the persistent alarm lives up to the "Anti-Mager" persona. I'll also clarify which tasks are overdue within the `CalendarView` modal. I am re-examining the overall prompt to ensure the final implementation perfectly aligns with expectations, and I'll be checking the CSS of `App.tsx`.


**Analyzing Animation Details**

I've examined the CSS for the shake animation, and it looks promising for the overdue notification. I want to confirm that the `CalendarView` modal correctly highlights "Overdue" tasks as expected. I'm also confirming the persistent alarm in this SPA will always be active because `App.tsx` has `isAlarmActive` state managing the overlay, regardless of what's currently in view. I will ensure no file truncation or Anti-Mager feel is lost in the re-upload.


**Finalizing App Refinements**

I'm making a final push to refine the app. I've updated `App.tsx` with a slightly more alarming two-tone sound for the persistent alarm and ensured a consistent vibration pattern. The global overlay's z-index is set to 150. In `CalendarView.tsx`, I've finalized clickable days and improved the modal for task details, clearly marking overdue tasks with a red border and text. The Gemini fallback message is also good. I am ready for the new build.

entries.description: Enhance the persistent alarm system with a more aggressive two-tone alert, ultra-high z-index for the overlay, and refined anti-mager messaging. Ensures the alarm is truly inescapable until dismissed.
Polished the CalendarView detailed task modal with better overdue indicators and mission category badges.
